name: Harvard University
meets_full_need_notes: Harvard meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students, domestic and international. Families with income under $85,000 pay nothing; under $150,000 typically pay no more than 10% of income.
no_loan_notes: Harvard eliminated loans from all financial aid packages in 2012. All Harvard scholarships are grants. Families with income under $85,000 contribute nothing; those above contribute based on income, assets, and expenses.
ed_aid_implications: Harvard's ED applicants receive the same need-based financial aid as RD. Aid is released with admission decision. Students may request release from ED if the aid offer is insufficient.
appeal_policy: Harvard reviews appeals for changed financial circumstances through their financial aid office. Students may request professional judgment review if family income or assets have changed significantly.
